Selected Record

Fourteen sectors covered with standards-based audit work and review-ready outputs.

This record focuses on audit proof: sector coverage, tasks performed, software exposure, file quality, and reporting responsibilities that can be defended in a recruiter or partner conversation.

03 // Proof of Work
Audit Coverage

Fourteen sectors covered with a consistent documentation standard.

Exposure spans banking, finance, IT, FDI, manufacturing, education, cooperative, private, listed, consulting, tax, law, costing, and NPO environments. The common requirement across them has been the same: execute cleanly, document fully, and report in a form suitable for management and partner review.

14 Sectors covered
NFRS / IFRS Standards context
verified

Review-ready outputs

Full audit documentation, reconciliations, evidence files, and reporting support materials are maintained in a form that can be reviewed, challenged, and followed up efficiently.

File quality proof

Execution strengths

  • Financial statement reviewFinancial statement review and analysis performed within standards-based reporting environments
  • Ledger analysisLedger review, reconciliations, evidence evaluation, and issue tracing across varied assignments
  • Audit supportPlanning support, testing, documentation handling, and partner reporting support across sector contexts
  • Issue documentationClear support files prepared for review, discussion, follow-up, and resolution

Sector record

Banking Finance Information Technology FDI Manufacturing Education Cooperative Private Listed Consulting Tax Law Costing NPOs

Software & Systems Exposure

This software exposure reflects real professional tooling used to support audit execution, reporting work, and file organization across varied assignments.

Banking systems exposure

Worked with confidential proprietary systems including CRM, CBS, and HR platforms used in banking environments, supporting evidence handling, process understanding, and review coordination.

Accounting and local workflow tools

Used local tools such as Tally and Rigo to support accounting review, reporting workflows, ledger handling, and structured follow-through in audit and finance contexts.

Documentation and review workflow

Software exposure has been used to keep audit files, supporting schedules, reconciliations, and reporting materials organized for management and partner oversight.

Key technical areas

Cross-sector assignments have strengthened practical awareness of accounting and audit matters that require stronger judgment, clearer support, and closer review.

Revenue recognition

Recognition issues were approached with attention to sector-specific operating models, support quality, and the link between underlying transactions and reported outcomes.

Expected Credit Loss (ECL)

Banking-related exposure strengthened awareness of ECL-sensitive processes, control environments, and the need for disciplined support around assumptions and review.

Provisions

Provision-related matters required clearer documentation of assumptions, review of supporting evidence, and communication suitable for management and partner discussion.

Sector-specific examples

Brief examples showing how sector context changes review focus, support requirements, and reporting emphasis.

Banking

Exposure includes expected credit loss considerations, control-sensitive workflows, and reporting structures that require disciplined support and clear documentation.

Manufacturing

Operational and inventory-led environments required closer attention to stock-related support, process understanding, and practical review of revenue and cost flows.

Listed and private entities

Different ownership and governance settings strengthened sensitivity to reporting quality, review expectations, and the presentation of support for management and partner discussion.

Built on evidence, clarity, and follow-through.

The core value is disciplined audit execution supported by broad sector awareness, strong file handling, and systems fluency that keeps review work moving.
